# fcs_environment_ingress (Resource)

Exposes a business cluster's service externally through the central FCS NAT-GW (DNAT on the cluster's existing public IP — no extra public IP is bound). All attributes force replacement; there is no in-place update. Destroy removes the DNAT rule and waits until it is reconciled away.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cluster_id` (String) ID of the business cluster to expose (must be active and have a public IP). Changing it forces a new ingress.
- `environment_id` (String) ID of the fcs_environment hosting the cluster. Changing it forces a new ingress.
- `hostname_prefix` (String) DNS label prefix; idempotency key per (environment, cluster). The reachable endpoint is the L4 public_url https://<public_ip>[:<port>]. Must match ^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?$ (max. 63 chars; no leading or trailing hyphen). Changing it forces a new ingress.
- `port` (Number) Target port (1–65535). Changing it forces a new ingress.
- `service` (String) Target service name inside the cluster (informational for the rule description). Changing it forces a new ingress.

### Optional

- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))
- `tls` (String) TLS handling: "auto" (the cluster ingress terminates TLS). Plain L7 host routing is not currently supported. Changing this value forces a new ingress.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Server-assigned ingress ID (UUID).
- `public_url` (String) Reachable L4 endpoint https://<public_ip>[:<port>] (null until known). Hostname-based L7 routing is planned for a future release.
- `status` (String) Lifecycle status: provisioning | offboarding | destroyed.

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) How long to wait for the create call to return (default 5m0s). Accepts a duration string such as "10m".
- `delete` (String) How long to wait for the teardown to finish (GET returns 404 or status=destroyed; default 15m0s).
